Not a validated digital twin; no evidence upgrade. FOUR genuinely-executed native reference runners now ship via puckworks/product/lab_runners.py, each reusing gate callables (no equation duplication), failure-isolated: waszkiewicz static refit, wadsworth permeability collapse, foster infiltration first-drip bracket (interactive-fast), and brewer2026.lb_reference (batch-only) — the LB plane-channel code-verification runner (simulated lattice permeability vs the exact plane-Poiseuille k=h^2/12; gate_lb_channel owns the 0.5% band via a shared channel_verification() helper; err_pct 0.052). NOT a generic bypass: the default/broad public batch selects the NOT_REVIEWED Cameron lens and still blocks before any producer; a mixed LB+unreviewed request blocks whole; the clearance never propagates; Grudeva stays RIGHTS_BLOCKED in every context (#73). This is numerical code verification, NOT coffee/espresso validation. A demonstrated second common-scenario adapter and affirmative Cameron/Grudeva reviews remain open (#70/#73). ACCESSIBILITY (#43): one rights-safe execution facade (puckworks.product.lab_service.execute_lab_request; explicit context, preflight before any producer, typed blocked result with no science) is consumed by the batch, the Colab notebook, and both Streamlit apps; a producer-free Explorer (puckworks.product.lab_explorer.explorer_catalog) exposes the full registry with zero execution; a form-driven novice Colab (notebooks/guided_pull_laboratory_colab.ipynb, one Run button, LOCAL_PRIVATE, commit-pinned dev preview, hermetic smoke asserts GUIDED_PULL_LAB_COMPLETE); and a public/local Streamlit split (apps/lab_public_app.py fixed to PUBLIC_ARTIFACT in code + apps/lab_app.py LOCAL_PRIVATE) with a producer-free Model library, cleared-only Component self-checks, and a disabled reference-shot pending Cameron's review.
